About

Building things that
actually work

I enjoy designing and building systems from the ground up — from circuit design and PCB layout to embedded software that brings hardware to life.

I'm always experimenting, prototyping, and refining ideas to create systems that are reliable, efficient, and practical in the real world.

01

Custom STM32F405 Development Board

Custom PCB projects spanning power management, sensor interfacing, and microcontroller breakout boards — from schematic capture to fabrication-ready Gerber files.

Altium DesignerPCB Layout & RoutingSchematic Design

02

NYU ARC Robotics:
Team Ultraviolet

Designing supercapacitor circuits with balance charging and full wiring harnesses for NYU's combat robotics competition team.

Altium DesignerLTSpiceWiring Soldering
View project
03

NYU Rogue Aerospace

Contributing to NYU’s competitive rocketry team by testing avionics firmware and validating flight controller systems for data logging, GPS tracking, and dual-deploy recovery.

AvionicsGPSArduino
View project
04

Lower Body Exoskeleton

Engineered a lower-limb exoskeleton with embedded motor control for ACL rehabilitation and enhance natural mobility. Implemented encoder-based actuation using L298N driver for stable knee and ankle support under variable loads.

Fusion 360ArduinoC/C++

Undergraduate Teaching Assistant

NYU General Engineering Department | Brooklyn, NY
